concepts.title: Properties of an odd and even function
concepts.conceptBody:
Properties of odd and even functions

(a) a constant function is an even function

(b) a zero function is both an odd and an even function.

(c) For two functions, the following are the rules for their respective operations.

(d) (i) if $\text{f}\left( \text{x} \right)+\text{f}\left( -\text{x} \right)=0\Rightarrow \text{f}$ is odd function.

(ii) if $\text{f}\left( \text{x} \right)-\text{f}\left( -\text{x} \right)=0\Rightarrow \text{f}$ is odd function.

(e) The derivative of an odd function is an even function and derivative of an even

function is an odd function.

(f) The square of even or an odd Function is always an even Function.

(g) Any function $y=f\left( x \right)$ can written as $y=f\left( x \right)=$ [odd part of $f\left( x \right)]+$ [even part of $f\left( x \right)]$

i.e. $\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }y=f\left( x \right)=\left[ \frac{f\left( x \right)-f\left( -x \right)}{2} \right]+\left[ \frac{f\left( x \right)+f\left( -x \right)}{2} \right]$

concepts.title: Determining odd and even functions
concepts.conceptBody:
Determining odd and even functions

Q) If $f\left( t ight)= rac{t}{{{e}^{t}}-1}+ rac{t}{2}+1$, show that $f\left( t ight)$ is an even function. 

Solution : 

Since $f\left( t ight)= rac{1}{{{e}^{t}}-1}+ rac{t}{2}+1$ …..(i)

Now, $f(-t)\,=\, rac{-t}{{{e}^{-t}}\,-\,1}\,-\, rac{t}{2}\,+1$

$=\, rac{t{{e}^{t}}}{{{e}^{t}}\,-\,1}- rac{t}{2}+1$ …..(ii)

Subtracting (ii) from (i), we get

$ egin{array}{*{35}{r}} f\left( t ight)-f\left( -t ight) & ~= rac{t}{\left( {{e}^{t}}-1 ight)}\left( 1-{{e}^{t}} ight)+t \ {} & ~=-t+t=0 \ \end{array}$

$ herefore ext{ }\!\!~\!\! ext{ f}\left( - ext{t} ight)- ext{f}\left( ext{t} ight)=0$. Hence $ ext{f}\left( ext{t} ight)$ is an even function.

Q) Find out whether the given function is even, odd or neither even nor odd. 

where, $f\left( x ight)=\left\{ egin{array}{*{35}{l}} x\left| x ight| & , ext{ }\!\!~\!\! ext{ }x\le -1 \ \left[ 1+x\left] + ight[1-x ight] & ,-1<x<1 \ -x\left| x ight| & , ext{ }\!\!~\!\! ext{ }x\ge 1 \ \end{array} ight\}$ 

where and [ ] represents modulus & greatest integer function. 

Solution : The given function can be written as :

$ egin{array}{*{35}{r}} {} & f\left( x ight)=\left\{ egin{align} & -\,{{x}^{2}}\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,,\,\,\,\,x\leq -1 \ & 2+[x]+[-x]\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,,\,\,\,-1<x<1 \ & -{{x}^{2}}\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,,\,\,x\,\geq \,1 \ \end{align} ight\} \ \end{array}$

$ egin{array}{*{35}{r}} {} & f\left( x ight)=\left\{ egin{array}{*{35}{l}} -{{x}^{2}} & , & x\leq -1 \ 2-1+0 & , & -1<x<0 \ 2 & , & x=0 \ 2+0-1 & , & 0<x<1 \ -{{x}^{2}} & , & x\geq 1 \ \end{array} ight\} \ \end{array}$

$f\left( x ight)=\left\{ egin{array}{*{35}{l}} -{{x}^{2}} & , & x\leq -1 \ 1 & , & -1<x<0 \ 2 & , & x=0 \ 1 & , & 0<x<1 \ {{x}^{2}} & , & x\geq 1 \ \end{array} ight\}$

which is clearly even as if $ ext{f}\left( - ext{x} ight)= ext{f}\left( ext{x} ight)$

Thus, $ ext{f}\left( ext{x} ight)$ is even.

Q) Find out whether the given function is even or odd function, where [ ] denotes greatest integer function. 

\(f(x)= rac{x(\sin x+ an x)}{\left[ rac{x+\pi}{\pi} ight]- rac{1}{2}}\), where \(x eq n \pi\)

Solution : 

$f\left( x ight)= rac{x\left( ext{sin}x+ ext{tan}x ight)}{\left[ rac{x+\pi }{\pi } ight] rac{-1}{2}}= rac{x\left( ext{sin}x+ ext{tan}x ight)}{\left[ rac{x}{\pi } ight]+1 rac{-1}{2}}$

$f\left( x ight)= rac{x\left( ext{sin}\, ext{x}+ ext{tan}\, ext{x} ight)}{\left[ rac{x}{\pi } ight]+0.5}$

$f\left( -x ight)= rac{-x\left( ext{sin}\left( -x ight)+ ext{tan}\left( -x ight) ight)}{\left[ rac{-x}{\pi } ight]+0.5}$

$f\left( -x ight)=\left\{ egin{array}{*{35}{l}} rac{x\left( ext{sin}x+ ext{tan}x ight)}{-1-\left[ rac{x}{\pi } ight]+0.5} & ,x e n\pi \ 0 & , ext{ }\!\!~\!\! ext{ }x=n\pi \ \end{array} ight\}$

$f\left( -x ight)=-\left( rac{x\left( ext{sin}x+ ext{tan}x ight)}{\left[ rac{x}{\pi } ight]+ rac{1}{2}} ight)$

$ ext{f}\left( - ext{x} ight)=- ext{f}\left( ext{x} ight)$

$ herefore ext{ }\!\!~\!\! ext{ }$ It is an odd function (if $ ext{x} e ext{n}\pi $ )

concepts.title: Odd and Even behaviour of composite functions
concepts.conceptBody:
Odd and Even behaviour of composite functions

  • If $\text{f}\left( \text{x} \right)\And \text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ g}\left( \text{x} \right)$ are even $\Rightarrow $ fog is an even function.
  • If $\text{f}\left( \text{x} \right)\And \text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ g}\left( \text{x} \right)$ are odd $\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }\Rightarrow $ fog is an odd function.
  • If $\text{f}\left( \text{x} \right)$ is even & $\text{g}\left( \text{x} \right)$ is odd $\Rightarrow \text{fog}$ is an even function.
  • If $\text{f}\left( \text{x} \right)$ is odd & $\text{g}\left( \text{x} \right)$ is even $\Rightarrow \text{fog}$ is an even function.
